note: ROADMAP — do in order, no skipping. Foundations done (micrograd, makemore 1-2, basic sim public). Steps t1-t4 are the WHOLE CORE: zero papers, a complete loop, and a real predict-vs-react RESULT lands at the end of t4; t5-t9 are deepening. The keystone gate is t2's plot (free-running vs teacher-forced error, vs horizon): flat -> prediction holds -> can plan (a planner; a startup); cliff -> can only react (a monitor; a commodity). Cost: t1-t7 ~$0 on the Mac, whole project <$2k — money is runway, not gear. Full per-step detail is in each task's notes; strategy / landscape / paper reading-order live in CLAUDE.md.

tasks.title: Log a dataset from the sim
tasks.notes: Many rollouts, randomized bank angles; save (state, action, next_state) tuples to a file. The sim becomes a DATA FACTORY — the training set for everything below. Do the read-step() / bank-angle tinker rep alongside (the judgment prerequisite). YOU ARE HERE.

tasks.title: Rung 1 — predict the next state (MLP) + the keystone plot
tasks.notes: THE FIRST KEYSTONE. Train an MLP (state,action)->next_state, MSE. Then the money plot: roll free-running vs teacher-forced, error vs horizon — THIS PLOT IS THE GO/NO-GO GATE (flat = can plan, cliff = can only react). Fixed field, memoryless, vario-only. Zero papers (pure makemore PyTorch). Barely-a-JEPA, but the loop closes — first real milestone.

tasks.title: Add a planner (CEM/MPC) + navigate to a goal
tasks.notes: Use rung-1's predictor to imagine action sequences, score them, pick the best. TASK = REACH A GOAL, not endurance (endurance is the reactive trap — greedy 'turn toward lift' wins it; navigation forces commitment). Field still fixed (baked into weights, no memory yet). Minimal decision-forcing world: two thermals + sink between + goal past the second. Now it tries to soar.

tasks.title: Compare to a reactive baseline — THE EXPERIMENT
tasks.notes: Reactive 'turn toward lift' vs the planner, same task. Headline: DOES PREDICTING BEAT REACTING? — the organizing question, as a number. Visible even on a fixed field: the reactor parks in thermal A or glides blindly; the planner commits to the A->B->goal route. (When airspeed becomes a control — a v2 upgrade — add MacCready speed-to-fly as the analytical optimum to score both against.)

tasks.title: Randomize + evolve the world (POMDP)
tasks.notes: Randomize layouts + thermal drift/decay (thermals move and fade). The agent can't memorize one map — it must INFER the current field from the lift it's felt -> the encoder gets a SHORT MEMORY WINDOW (the POMDP fix). Re-run the keystone and watch it get harder — isolates difficulty-in-the-world from difficulty-in-the-glider. Drift/decay is predictable (JEPA's home turf); a thermal being born is priors-only — step 5 tests the predictable kind. BUILD THE SENSOR FIREWALL HERE.

tasks.title: Rung 2 — the real JEPA (latent prediction)
tasks.notes: THE REAL JEPA. Swap predict-next-state for encode -> predict next LATENT, with EMA target encoder + stop-gradient + anti-collapse. Papers read just-in-time: BYOL -> I-JEPA -> V-JEPA 2 / 2-AC -> LeWM (full annotated list = CLAUDE.md Reading order). Re-run keystone + planner; compare latent-JEPA vs rung-1. HONEST HEADS-UP: on a low-dim state rung 2 may NOT dramatically beat rung 1 — its payoff is learning the mechanism on a safe problem + being the prerequisite for vision. Don't read a tie as failure.

tasks.title: Dynamic soaring (shear layer)
tasks.notes: Same engine, swap the thermal field for a SHEAR LAYER. The albatross energy-extraction loop is a committed trajectory you can't react into -> the hard test of plan-through. Thermal centering was the gentle test; here prediction's advantage should have real teeth.

tasks.title: (Optional) Vision branch — add the camera
tasks.notes: Brings a renderer (clouds/terrain) + a vision encoder (ViT/CNN) + V-JEPA-2-load onto the path. Answers: does SEEING the clouds beat inferring from the trail? — measured against the vario-only baseline. Gated behind everything above (it re-scopes the syllabus, so NOT next).

tasks.title: (Optional) Real hardware — RC glider/car, inference only
tasks.notes: RC glider + flight controller + vario/IMU/GPS, INFERENCE ONLY (training stays on the Mac). A second, filmable, real-physics datapoint. Not needed for the result. The hobby-scan flagged the RC CAR as the cheapest physical keystone if a glider is fiddly — same engine on wheels, also filmable.

tasks.title: Move CLIs into a cli/ folder for gen_docs auto-discovery
tasks.notes: Move CLI entrypoints into a dedicated cli/ folder so gen_docs.py globs cli/*.py instead of the hardcoded CLIS list — makes 'every CLI is documented' structural, killing the drift class the judge flagged (menu.py was silently missing from the generated ref, and docs-generated can't catch its own omission). Touches: hook paths (.githooks/, .claude/settings.json), the scripts/ test-exempt entry, land.py's sibling import of check_all, and every doc referencing scripts/<name>.py. Own branch; don't fold into other work.

tasks.title: land.py should commit the done-marking, not leave it dirty
tasks.notes: task.py done() only mutates task_list.json on disk; land.py step 6b never commits that edit, so main is left dirty after every land until someone happens to commit it. Surfaced when t10's done-mark got stranded uncommitted inside a concurrent session's WIP checkout. Fix: land.py commits + pushes the task_list.json update itself, atomically, inside the flock.

tasks.title: land.py must authorize its own done-mark commit (ALLOW_MAIN_COMMIT)
tasks.notes: t11's fix made land.py 'git commit' the done-mark on main, but the pre-commit hook refuses direct commits to main and land.py never set ALLOW_MAIN_COMMIT for itself -- so the very first real land under the t11 fix silently failed to commit (best-effort, so LANDED still printed), reproducing the exact bug t11 was fixing, one level deeper. Fix: land.py sets ALLOW_MAIN_COMMIT=1 process-wide alongside the existing LAND_ACTIVE=1, same self-authorization pattern already used for the merge hook.

tasks.title: Flight replay viewport (pygame) — scrub/step/play logged episodes + instruments
tasks.notes: REPLAY-ONLY pygame viewport for logged flight episodes (NOT realtime manual flight — that is a future task built on top of this one). Features: episode selector; play/pause + playback speed; timeline scrub; single-step forward/back; top-down view (thermal-field heatmap + flown path + glider marker + heading); instrument panel (airspeed, altimeter, vario, vario_te, bank). Scrubbing = pure array indexing — episodes are precomputed contiguous chains; NO physics runs in the viewport. DATA: data/dataset.npz (gitignored; regenerate with .venv/bin/python data_gen.py). Self-describing format: true_states (N,6: x,y,z,heading,airspeed,bank), sensors (N,8: same 6 + vario,vario_te), actions (N,2: bank_cmd,pitch_cmd), episode (N,) rollout ids, plus in-file name arrays, glider_params(+names), dt, seed. Within an episode true_next_states[i]==true_states[i+1] bit-exact. Draw the field via data_gen.make_world() (fixed-field phase) — thermal truth IS allowed for human eyes; the sensor firewall only forbids it as MODEL input. Naming convention everywhere: _cmd=asked, bare=actual, true_*=answer key (eval only). WHY (from main-session discussion 2026-07-01): (a) Mode-B silent-failure detector — watching rollouts catches what numbers hide (stalls, mush, weird arcs); (b) at t2 this gains ghost-replay: model-predicted trajectory overlaid on truth, diverging over horizon = the keystone plot as a movie — design the renderer to accept a SECOND trajectory cheaply now; (c) one log format for ALL pilots (random/planner/JEPA/human) is guaranteed by construction — every pilot flies through sim.step(bank_cmd, pitch_cmd); add a per-episode pilot-provenance tag only when a second pilot type exists (npz keys are additive, no format break). CONTEXT TRAIL: progress.txt (decision log incl. physics-fold deferrals), CLAUDE.md section Sim-v2-scoping (viewport rec: 2D pygame first, scope caution — polish on purpose, serve the JEPA), glider_sim.py module docstring (physics + panel + naming), data_gen.py docstring (schema + firewall). Left-off state: t1 (real airframe physics + data factory) green at handoff on feature/t1-dataset awaiting land greenlight. pygame must be added as a dependency (uv).

tasks.title: task.py add commits id allocation to main under land.py's lock
tasks.notes: task.py add's _next_id() reads whatever task_list.json happens to be in the calling worktree; under worktree-per-task, two sessions adding around the same time independently compute the same next id (hit for real: two sessions both got t11). Fix: task.py add resolves task_list.json from main's actual tip (fetch/read main ref, not the local worktree copy), acquires the SAME queue.lock land.py uses so two adds can never race between read-next-id and claim-it, and commits the new entry directly onto local main (self-authorized like land.py's ALLOW_MAIN_COMMIT) -- but does NOT push, so publishing stays land.py's job on the user's greenlight. Then copies the updated file back into the calling worktree so the id is visible immediately.

tasks.title: task.py list hides done tasks by default
tasks.notes: task.py list currently prints every task including done ones, growing unbounded as the roadmap progresses. Per user preference (2026-07-02): hide done tasks from the default listing; -v/--full (already the 'show more' flag) also brings them back into view. rehydrate.py already only shows a done COUNT, not individual done tasks -- no change needed there. The header progress bar / counts stay based on the full task list regardless of what's filtered from the per-row listing.

tasks.title: Wingtip lift-asymmetry sensor — the bird gradient cue (before t2)
tasks.notes: sense() gains lift_asym = updraft(left wingtip) - updraft(right wingtip): the instantaneous LATERAL lift gradient — the torque cue birds center thermals with (validated: Reddy et al. PNAS 2016 + Nature 2018 — torque cue essential, vario-only much weaker). Glider gains wingspan (~17 m, ASK-21); wingtip horizontal separation scales with cos(bank) (knife-edge = no lateral sampling). Firewall-legal: field sampled at aircraft-attached points — felt, never told. Point-mass dynamics UNCHANGED (cue is sensed, not a simulated torque). SENSOR_NAMES grows to 9 — data_gen packs off SENSOR_NAMES so the factory adapts automatically; regenerate data/dataset.npz after landing. Sign: positive = left wing in stronger lift = turn left. Why before t2: first dataset the MLP sees should carry the bird sensor; also gives t4's reactive baseline the honest bird rule (turn toward the lifting wing) so predict-vs-react is fair. NOTE: t13 viewport notes say sensors (N,8) — stale; format is self-describing, read sensor_names from the file.

tasks.title: task.py notes command — edit a task's notes after creation
tasks.notes: add/block/done can't fix a typo or add context to an existing task's notes without ALLOW_STATE_EDIT. Add 'task.py notes <id> (--set TEXT | --append TEXT)' following start/done/block's local-save pattern (no main-commit needed, land.py reconciles).
